title: Trichocline spathulata
taxonomy: Trichocline spathulata is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It belongs to the tribe Mutisieae, a group of plants that share similar characteristics and are often found in temperate regions. The species is also classified as a member of the genus Trichocline, which is native to New Zealand. Trichocline spathulata is a specific species within this genus and is often found in the wild, growing in a variety of habitats. Further classification of the species is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales.
uses: Trichocline spathulata is a plant species native to New Zealand, and while there is limited information available on its medicinal uses, it is said to have potential health benefits. In traditional Māori medicine, the plant has been used for various purposes, including as a treatment for fever and to aid in wound healing. The leaves and stems of Trichocline spathulata are also thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in treating conditions such as arthritis.
